# Output

Files in this directory are generated by `python run.py`. The directory can be regenerated from the supplied example input, normalization scalers and pretrained checkpoint.

## Generated files

| File | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `input_hic.npy` | Hi-C matrix used as the generation condition |
| `reference_dna_fish_centroid_um.npy` | Experimental DNA-FISH ensemble centroid used for evaluation |
| `generated_single_cell_distances_um.npy` | Generated single-cell distance matrices, shaped `(n, 50, 50)`, in micrometres |
| `generated_ensemble_centroid_um.npy` | Mean of the generated single-cell distance matrices |
| `fish_centroid_relative_distance.npy` | Experimental centroid after within-matrix scale normalization for shape visualization |
| `generated_centroid_relative_distance.npy` | Generated centroid after within-matrix scale normalization for shape visualization |
| `fish_centroid_relative_coordinates.npy` | Three-dimensional MDS coordinates for the experimental centroid |
| `generated_centroid_relative_coordinates.npy` | Aligned three-dimensional MDS coordinates for the generated centroid |
| `centroid_3d_comparison.html` | Interactive Plotly comparison of the two relative centroid geometries |
| `demo_summary.json` | Sampling settings, metrics, validity checks and visualization metadata |

## Interpretation

`generated_single_cell_distances_um.npy` and `generated_ensemble_centroid_um.npy` retain physical distance units and should be used for quantitative distance analyses.

The files containing `relative` in their names are used only for shape visualization. Each centroid distance matrix is divided by its own median lower-triangular distance before MDS, and each coordinate configuration is normalized to unit radius of gyration. These coordinates are dimensionless and should not be interpreted as absolute micrometre-scale reconstructions.

The experimental centroid is not used to select generated cells. It is included only for ensemble-level evaluation and visualization.
